GameVictory.summary: Triggers the victory screen for the player.
GameVictory.tip: Use this at the end of your final quest to complete the map. Make sure all critical objectives are done before calling it.

GameLose.summary: Triggers the defeat screen for the player.
GameLose.tip: Commonly used inside ObjectLose or HeroKill triggers to enforce fail conditions.

Dialog.summary: Shows a story dialog by key.

params.0: Dialog key — must match a dialog ID in your map ZIP's DB/dialogs/ folder.

Dialog.tip: The dialog JSON and its localization tokens must be included in your map's companion ZIP. Use Export ZIP to bundle them.

RandomDialog.summary: Plays a randomly chosen dialog from the given list of keys.
RandomDialog.tip: Good for giving NPCs variety. All listed dialogs must exist in your map ZIP.

Print.summary: Prints a debug or achievement message.
Print.tip: Mainly used for debugging during development. The message appears in the game log.

CurrentSubQuestDone.summary: Marks the currently active subquest as complete.
CurrentSubQuestDone.tip: Use this instead of SubQuestDone when you're inside the subquest's own trigger — it's simpler and avoids hardcoding quest IDs.

SubQuestActivate.summary: Activates a specific subquest on a quest.

params.0: Quest SID — the quest that owns the subquest.
params.1: Subquest SID or index — the subquest to activate.

SubQuestActivate.tip: Use to unlock a side quest from a different quest's trigger. For linear progression, prefer NextSubQuest.

SubQuestDeactivate.summary: Deactivates a specific subquest on a quest.

NextQuest.summary: Advances to the next quest in a series.

params.0: Quest SID — the quest to advance.

EndQuest.tip: The quest disappears from the quest log. Use after all subquests are done.

NextSubQuest.summary: Activates the next subquest by SID or numeric index.

SubQuestDone.summary: Marks a specific subquest as done from outside its own quest.
SubQuestDone.tip: Use when you need one quest to complete a subquest in another quest.

NextAfterGroup.summary: Activates the next subquest once all subquests in a group are done.
NextAfterGroup.tip: Useful for optional side objectives — wait for all of them before continuing the main line.

CounterSet.summary: Sets a counter to a specific value.
CounterSet.tip: Counters must be declared in the Counters tab before use.

CounterPlus.summary: Increments a counter by the given amount.

CounterMinus.summary: Decrements a counter by the given amount.

CounterSetRandom.summary: Sets a counter to a random integer between min and max (inclusive).

GiveUnitHero.summary: Adds units to a hero's army.

params.0: Unit SID — creature type to give.

GiveUnitHero.tip: Units are added on top of what the hero already has. If the hero has no free slots, units go into the first available slot.

GiveUnitHeroPerWeek.summary: Adds units to a hero's army each week.
GiveUnitHeroPerWeek.tip: This repeats automatically. Put it in a StartWeek trigger with no conditions, or guard it with a Counter if you want it to stop.

RemoveUnitHero.summary: Removes units from a hero's army.

params.1: Count to remove, or 'all' to remove all units of that type.

GiveStatsHero.summary: Adds stat points to a hero.

params.0: Stat name: offence, defence, spellPower, intelligence, movementBonus, or moral.

GiveExpHero.summary: Gives experience points to a hero.

params.1: Hero SID (optional — omit to give to the current player hero).

GiveItemHero.summary: Gives an artifact to a hero.
GiveItemHero.tip: The hero must exist on the map. If the hero's backpack is full, the item may be lost.

AddSkillHero.summary: Adds one level of a skill to a hero.
AddSkillHero.tip: Call this action multiple times to add multiple levels of the same skill.

AddSpellHero.summary: Teaches a spell to a hero.

AddBuffHeroDays.summary: Applies a buff to a hero for N days.

params.2: Duration in days. Use -1 for a permanent buff.

RemoveBuffHero.summary: Removes a buff from a hero.

params.1: Hero SID (optional — leave blank to remove from all heroes).

HeroResetMovePointsMax.summary: Resets a hero's movement points to their maximum.
HeroResetMovePointsMax.tip: Useful after HeroStop or at the start of a cutscene sequence.

SpawnHero.summary: Spawns a hero on the map at a given node for a specific player.

params.1: Node ID — map node to spawn at.

DeleteHero.summary: Removes a hero from the map permanently.
DeleteHero.tip: This does not trigger HeroKill conditions.

DisableAiHero.summary: Disables AI control for a hero.

TeleportHero.summary: Teleports a hero instantly to a map node.

HeroToNode.summary: Moves a hero toward a map node (pathfinding, not instant).

HeroToHero.summary: Moves one hero toward another using pathfinding.

HeroStop.summary: Stops the hero's current movement path.
HeroStop.tip: Call this before starting a cutscene or important scripted sequence to prevent the hero from wandering off.

ChangeManaHero.summary: Sets a hero's mana to the given value.

SetHeroMovePoints.summary: Sets a hero's movement points to a specific value.

ResurrectHero.summary: Resurrects a defeated hero at a map node.

GiveRes.summary: Gives the player a specified amount of a resource.

params.0: Resource type: gold, wood, ore, crystals, mercury, gemstones, or dust.

RemoveRes.summary: Removes a resource from the player's stockpile.
RemoveRes.tip: Does not check if the player has enough — removing more than available sets the resource to 0.

MoveCamera.summary: Moves the camera to focus on a map node.

RevealFogOfWar.summary: Reveals fog of war around a node.

DeleteEntity.summary: Removes a map entity (blocking object, decoration, zone) permanently.

SpawnObject.summary: Spawns an interactable object at a node.

params.3: Entity SID to assign (optional, used to reference the object later).

SpawnMapObject.summary: Spawns a map decoration or non-interactable object at a node.

params.1: true to enable, false to disable.

EntityActionsOff.summary: Disables all interactions on a map entity (makes it non-clickable).

SetQuestMarker.summary: Attaches a quest marker VFX to a map entity.

SetActiveQuestMarker.summary: Shows or hides a quest marker already attached to an entity.

params.1: true to show, false to hide.

CreateVFX.summary: Creates a VFX effect at a node.

params.4: Entity SID to assign (optional, allows toggling via SetActiveVFX).

SetActiveVFX.summary: Shows or hides a VFX entity.

params.1: true to show, false to hide.

SetActiveMarker.summary: Shows or hides a zone or interaction marker.

params.1: true to show, false to hide.
Upgrade string in 'tier,level' format (e.g. '0,2' = upgrade by 2 levels).

DeleteSquad.summary: Removes a neutral squad from the map.

TriggerClearCustom.summary: Clears/resets a specific trigger so it can fire again.
TriggerClearCustom.tip: Useful for repeating triggers that normally fire only once.

ChangeCampaignOneStep.summary: Advances the campaign by one step.

AiBanArea.summary: Prohibits AI heroes from entering the area around a node.

AiUnbanArea.summary: Lifts an AI movement ban from the area around a node.

StartTurn.summary: Fires at the start of a specific turn for a specific player.

params.0: Turn number (optional — omit to fire every turn).
params.1: Player index (optional — omit for any player).

StartTurn.tip: With no parameters, this fires at the start of every turn for every player. Use the Counter extra field to require N turns have passed.

StartWeek.summary: Fires at the start of any week.
StartWeek.tip: Weeks are every 7 turns. Use this for periodic events like weekly reinforcements.

AnyStartTurn.summary: Fires at the start of any player's turn.
AnyStartTurn.tip: More permissive than StartTurn. Typically used with Counter conditions to time events.

Counter.summary: Checks a counter's value against a threshold.

params.0: Counter SID — the counter to check.

Counter.tip: Counters must be declared in the Counters tab. This condition is usually combined with others using AND logic.

HeroKill.summary: Fires when the specified hero is killed in combat.
HeroKill.tip: Fires when the hero is defeated, not necessarily removed from the map. Pair with GameLose for defeat conditions.

SquadKill.summary: Fires when a specific neutral squad is defeated.

params.0: Squad entity SID — the neutral stack on the map.

SquadKill.tip: The squad SID is set in the map editor when you place the neutral stack, not the unit type SID.

ObjectLose.summary: Fires when the player loses a specific map object (e.g. a town).

ObjectInteractionBefore.summary: Fires before the player interacts with a map object.
ObjectInteractionBefore.tip: Use 'Before' when you want to intercept the interaction and show a dialog before the default event fires.

ObjectInteractionAfter.summary: Fires after the player interacts with a map object.

ObjectCaptureEntity.summary: Fires when the player captures a map entity (mine, city, etc.).

NodeRevealed.summary: Fires when a specific map node is revealed (fog of war lifted).

SquadInteraction.summary: Fires when the player interacts with (approaches) a neutral squad.
SquadInteraction.tip: Triggers before combat starts. Useful for dialog-before-battle sequences.

UnitOwnSide.summary: Checks how many units of a type the player's side owns across all armies.

UnitOwnHero.summary: Checks how many units of a type a specific hero owns.

UnitKill.summary: Fires when a unit type is killed.
UnitKill.tip: Use the Counter extra field to require N kills before this condition fires (e.g. kill 10 goblins).

UnitLose.summary: Fires when a unit type is lost (killed on your side).
UnitLose.tip: Use the Counter extra field to set a loss threshold.

UnitHire.summary: Fires when a unit type is recruited/hired.
UnitHire.tip: Use the Counter extra field to require N hires.

ResCounter.summary: Checks the player's resource stockpile against a value.

params.0: Resource type: gold, wood, ore, crystals, mercury, gemstones, dust.

ItemOwnSide.summary: Checks whether the player's side owns a specific artifact.

params.2: Count (usually 0 for 'doesn't have', 1 for 'has at least one').

DifficultyCustomMap.summary: Checks the custom map difficulty setting.

params.0: Type: Neutral (neutral strength), Economy (starting resources), or Ai (AI strength).

DifficultyCustomMap.tip: Use this to scale enemy strength or rewards per difficulty tier.

PlayerDefeated.summary: Fires when a specific player is defeated.

fields.quest.name: Localization SID shown as the quest name in the in-game quest log. Must have a matching token in customMaps.json.
fields.quest.description: Localization SID shown as the quest description. Must have a matching token in customMaps.json.
fields.trigger.isRepeating: If true, this trigger fires every time its conditions are met, not just the first time. Use carefully — unguarded repeating triggers can fire many times per turn.
fields.counter.sid: Unique identifier for this counter. Used in CounterSet, CounterPlus, CounterMinus, and Counter conditions.
fields.counter.value: Initial value of the counter when the map loads. Usually 0.
fields.interruption.sid: Unique identifier for this interruption. Used by EnableInterruption and DisableInterruption actions.
